odeComponent.htmlView:
In this project, students will create and perform a small play in English for younger children in Kindergarten. The theme is about theatre, stories, animals, emotions, and daily routines.

The students will work in groups and each person will have a role, like writer, actor, or video helper. First, they will write a simple script in English. Then, they will practise speaking, pronunciation, and acting. Finally, they will perform the play and record it on video to share with the school.

During the project, students will improve their English speaking and writing skills, learn to work as a team, and use technology in a fun way.

The project will last about 3 weeks, with 8 to 10 lessons. It connects English, Art, and Spanish Language classes.

The main goal is to learn English in a creative and real situation while having fun and helping younger students enjoy theatre.

Some applications to use in class: Symbaloo
odeComponent.jsonProperties:
{"ideviceId":"idevice-1778165961849-upqrxs5ti","textInfoDurationInput":"","textInfoDurationTextInput":"Duración","textInfoParticipantsInput":"","textInfoParticipantsTextInput":"Agrupamiento","textTextarea":"

In this project, students will create and perform a small play in English for younger children in Kindergarten. The theme is about theatre, stories, animals, emotions, and daily routines.

The students will work in groups and each person will have a role, like writer, actor, or video helper. First, they will write a simple script in English. Then, they will practise speaking, pronunciation, and acting. Finally, they will perform the play and record it on video to share with the school.

During the project, students will improve their English speaking and writing skills, learn to work as a team, and use technology in a fun way.

The project will last about 3 weeks, with 8 to 10 lessons. It connects English, Art, and Spanish Language classes.

The main goal is to learn English in a creative and real situation while having fun and helping younger students enjoy theatre.

Some applications to use in class: Symbaloo

","textFeedbackInput":"Show Feedback","textFeedbackTextarea":""}

odeComponent.htmlView:
LET’S ACT! 
First, we are going to watch two examples of children’s theatre plays in English.

PADLET: LOOK FOR THEATRE EXAMPLES

While you watch, think about these questions:

Who are the characters?
What happens in the story?
What English words do you understand?

Your Mission
At the end of this project, you will create your own theatre play in English and at the end, we will perform in 5 years class. Also we will record your theatre play for the school.

Ready to become actors and actresses?
Let’s watch, learn and have fun together!
odeComponent.jsonProperties:
{"ideviceId":"idevice-1778151011508-07oljv39r","textInfoDurationInput":"","textInfoDurationTextInput":"Duración","textInfoParticipantsInput":"","textInfoParticipantsTextInput":"Agrupamiento","textTextarea":"

LET’S ACT! 
First, we are going to watch two examples of children’s theatre plays in English.

PADLET: LOOK FOR THEATRE EXAMPLES

While you watch, think about these questions:

Who are the characters?
What happens in the story?
What English words do you understand?

Your Mission
At the end of this project, you will create your own theatre play in English and at the end, we will perform in 5 years class. Also we will record your theatre play for the school.

Ready to become actors and actresses?
Let’s watch, learn and have fun together!

","textFeedbackInput":"Show Feedback","textFeedbackTextarea":""}

odeComponent.htmlView:
Today we are going to share ideas for our theatre project using Mentimeter.

- What do you have to do?
 1º Wait for your teacher to give you the Mentimeter code.
2º Enter the code on the website.
3º Write your ideas for the project.
4º You can think about: Characters, stories, animals, costumes, decorations, funny moments, english words or expressions

Important!
Respect other people’s ideas.
Write short and clear answers.
Think carefully before writing.
Reflection Time
Before writing your ideas, think about these questions:

- What kind of story would you like to create?
- What characters could appear in the theatre play?
- What would make the play fun for little children?
- How can we work well as a team?


Your ideas will help us create our final theatre project together!
odeComponent.jsonProperties:
{"ideviceId":"idevice-1778153232347-wricxcddu","textInfoDurationInput":"","textInfoDurationTextInput":"Duración","textInfoParticipantsInput":"","textInfoParticipantsTextInput":"Agrupamiento","textTextarea":"

Today we are going to share ideas for our theatre project using Mentimeter.

- What do you have to do?
 1º Wait for your teacher to give you the Mentimeter code.
2º Enter the code on the website.
3º Write your ideas for the project.
4º You can think about: Characters, stories, animals, costumes, decorations, funny moments, english words or expressions

Important!
Respect other people’s ideas.
Write short and clear answers.
Think carefully before writing.
Reflection Time
Before writing your ideas, think about these questions:

- What kind of story would you like to create?
- What characters could appear in the theatre play?
- What would make the play fun for little children?
- How can we work well as a team?


Your ideas will help us create our final theatre project together!

","textFeedbackInput":"Show Feedback","textFeedbackTextarea":""}

odeComponent.htmlView:
Today you are going to start writing your theatre script in English with your group.

What do you have to do?
- Work together with your team.
- Create a short and simple story.
- Write the dialogues for the characters.
- Use easy English sentences and vocabulary.
- Think about the beginning, middle and ending of the story.

You will use:
Google Docs 

On-line Dictionary - wordreference

Google Classroom


Remember!
- Everyone in the group must participate.
- Help your classmates if they need support.
- Use polite words and respect other ideas.
- Check spelling and punctuation before finishing.
- Think about these questions:

     - Who are the characters?
     - Where does the story happen?
     - What problem or adventure is in the story?
     - How does the story end?
     - Will little children understand your play?

Your goal
Create a fun and clear theatre script in English for the children in PrePrimary Education.
odeComponent.jsonProperties:
{"ideviceId":"idevice-1778153478034-vuxbklytj","textInfoDurationInput":"","textInfoDurationTextInput":"Duración","textInfoParticipantsInput":"","textInfoParticipantsTextInput":"Agrupamiento","textTextarea":"

Today you are going to start writing your theatre script in English with your group.

What do you have to do?
- Work together with your team.
- Create a short and simple story.
- Write the dialogues for the characters.
- Use easy English sentences and vocabulary.
- Think about the beginning, middle and ending of the story.

You will use:
Google Docs 

On-line Dictionary - wordreference

Google Classroom


Remember!
- Everyone in the group must participate.
- Help your classmates if they need support.
- Use polite words and respect other ideas.
- Check spelling and punctuation before finishing.
- Think about these questions:

     - Who are the characters?
     - Where does the story happen?
     - What problem or adventure is in the story?
     - How does the story end?
     - Will little children understand your play?

Your goal
Create a fun and clear theatre script in English for the children in PrePrimary Education.

","textFeedbackInput":"Show Feedback","textFeedbackTextarea":""}

odeComponent.htmlView:
What do you have to do?
- Decide who will play each character.
- Read the script together.
- Practise your dialogues in English.
- Speak clearly and loudly.
- Help your team during the rehearsal.

You will use:
- Your digital script

- Audio recorder

- ELSA Speaks

- Classroom devices

Remember!
- Respect all classmates and their ideas.
- Everyone must participate.
- Practise pronunciation and expression.
- Do not worry about mistakes — we are learning together.
- Think about these questions:
- Does your character sound happy, sad or excited?
- Can the audience understand you clearly?
- Are you speaking with confidence?
- How can your group improve the play?

Your goal
Work together to organise the theatre play and improve your speaking in English.

- Decide who will play each character.
- Read the script together.
- Practise your dialogues in English.
- Speak clearly and loudly.
- Help your team during the rehearsal.

You will use:
- Your digital script

- Audio recorder

- ELSA Speaks

- Classroom devices

Remember!
- Respect all classmates and their ideas.
- Everyone must participate.
- Practise pronunciation and expression.
- Do not worry about mistakes — we are learning together.
- Think about these questions:
- Does your character sound happy, sad or excited?
- Can the audience understand you clearly?
- Are you speaking with confidence?
- How can your group improve the play?

Your goal
Work together to organise the theatre play and improve your speaking in English.

","textFeedbackInput":"Show Feedback","textFeedbackTextarea":""}

odeComponent.htmlView:
For teachers only: here you can find the connection between the different tasks, the LOMLOE assessment criteria, materials, applications, and more.

Here you can also find the project timeline- PADLET TIMELINE

In the “Self-assessment” section, you can find the self-evaluation form that I have included so that students can assess themselves. I also include a link to the checklist.

\n

Here you can also find the project timeline- PADLET TIMELINE

\n

In the “Self-assessment” section, you can find the self-evaluation form that I have included so that students can assess themselves. I also include a link to the checklist.

odeComponent.htmlView:
What do you have to do?
- Work with your group.
- Design backgrounds, posters or decorations for the play.
- Choose colours, images and titles carefully.
- Make your theatre attractive and easy to understand for kids.

You will use:
Canva
Classroom devices or computers

Remember!
- Everyone in the group must participate.
- Respect your classmates’ ideas.
- Keep your design clear and creative.
- Use simple English words if needed.

Think about these questions:
- Does your background match the story?
- Is the design colourful and interesting?
- Will young children understand the images?
- Are all group members helping?

Your goal
Create creative digital materials to make your theatre play more fun and visual.
odeComponent.jsonProperties:
{"ideviceId":"idevice-1778163769959-kxoguy64c","textInfoDurationInput":"","textInfoDurationTextInput":"Duración","textInfoParticipantsInput":"","textInfoParticipantsTextInput":"Agrupamiento","textTextarea":"

What do you have to do?
- Work with your group.
- Design backgrounds, posters or decorations for the play.
- Choose colours, images and titles carefully.
- Make your theatre attractive and easy to understand for kids.

You will use:
Canva
Classroom devices or computers

Remember!
- Everyone in the group must participate.
- Respect your classmates’ ideas.
- Keep your design clear and creative.
- Use simple English words if needed.

Think about these questions:
- Does your background match the story?
- Is the design colourful and interesting?
- Will young children understand the images?
- Are all group members helping?

Your goal
Create creative digital materials to make your theatre play more fun and visual.

","textFeedbackInput":"Show Feedback","textFeedbackTextarea":""}

odeComponent.htmlView:
Today you are going to record your final theatre performance in English.

What do you have to do?
- Work together with your group.
- Act out your theatre play.
- Speak clearly and loudly in English.
- Use your expressions, movements and costumes.
- Try your best and enjoy the performance.

You will use:
- Tablet, camera or mobile phone
- Your theatre materials and decorations

Remember!
- Respect your classmates during the recording.
- Listen carefully and wait for your turn.
- Do not worry if you make small mistakes.
- Help your group if someone needs support.

Think about these questions:
- Can the audience understand your English?
- Are you speaking with confidence?
- Are you using good expression and body language?
- Is your group working well together?

Your goal
Communicate in English through a real theatre performance for the school community.
odeComponent.jsonProperties:
{"ideviceId":"idevice-1778163984753-sqbp1n71b","textInfoDurationInput":"","textInfoDurationTextInput":"Duración","textInfoParticipantsInput":"","textInfoParticipantsTextInput":"Agrupamiento","textTextarea":"

Today you are going to record your final theatre performance in English.

What do you have to do?
- Work together with your group.
- Act out your theatre play.
- Speak clearly and loudly in English.
- Use your expressions, movements and costumes.
- Try your best and enjoy the performance.

You will use:
- Tablet, camera or mobile phone
- Your theatre materials and decorations

Remember!
- Respect your classmates during the recording.
- Listen carefully and wait for your turn.
- Do not worry if you make small mistakes.
- Help your group if someone needs support.

Think about these questions:
- Can the audience understand your English?
- Are you speaking with confidence?
- Are you using good expression and body language?
- Is your group working well together?

Your goal
Communicate in English through a real theatre performance for the school community.

","textFeedbackInput":"Show Feedback","textFeedbackTextarea":""}

odeComponent.htmlView:
Today you are going to edit your final theatre video with your group.

What do you have to do?
- Work together with your team.
- Add scenes, titles or music to the video.
- Choose the best parts of your recording.
- Make the video clear, creative and fun to watch.

You will use:
- CapCut 
- Classroom devices

Remember!
- Everyone in the group must participate.
- Respect your classmates’ ideas.
- Use the digital tools carefully and responsibly.
- Keep the video simple and organised.

Your goal
Create a final digital video of your theatre play using multimedia tools.
odeComponent.jsonProperties:
{"ideviceId":"idevice-1778164486662-0thrgzcnd","textInfoDurationInput":"","textInfoDurationTextInput":"Duración","textInfoParticipantsInput":"","textInfoParticipantsTextInput":"Agrupamiento","textTextarea":"

Today you are going to edit your final theatre video with your group.

What do you have to do?
- Work together with your team.
- Add scenes, titles or music to the video.
- Choose the best parts of your recording.
- Make the video clear, creative and fun to watch.

You will use:
- CapCut 
- Classroom devices

Remember!
- Everyone in the group must participate.
- Respect your classmates’ ideas.
- Use the digital tools carefully and responsibly.
- Keep the video simple and organised.

Your goal
Create a final digital video of your theatre play using multimedia tools.

","textFeedbackInput":"Show Feedback","textFeedbackTextarea":""}

odeComponent.htmlView:
Today you are going to present your theatre play to the children in Infant Education.

What do you have to do?
- Watch and present your final theatre video.

You will use:
- Video
- Projector

Remember!
- Be respectful and responsible during the presentation.
- Listen carefully when others are speaking.
- Show a positive attitude and teamwork.
- Feel proud of your work and effort.

Think about these questions:
- Can the audience understand the story?
- Is your English clear and understandable?
- How do younger children react to the play?
- What have you learned during this project?


Your goal
Share your theatre play with a real audience using English in a meaningful and communicative way.

Your goal

","textFeedbackInput":"Show Feedback","textFeedbackTextarea":""}
